items.title: 📦 vllm-project/vllm v0.16.0
items.raw_summary: # vLLM v0.16.0  ## Highlights  This release features 440 commits from 203 contributors (7 new)!  * **Async scheduling + Pipeline Parallelism** is now fully supported, delivering **30.8% E2E throughput improvement** and **31.8% TPOT improvement** (#32618). * **Realtime API**: A ne
items.why_it_matters: vLLM v0.16.0 delivers 30.8% throughput improvement via async scheduling and pipeline parallelism, plus a new Realtime API — a major upgrade for anyone serving LLMs in production.
items.action: Upgrade vLLM and benchmark the async scheduling gains

items.title: In November, we outlined our approach to deprecating and preserving older Claude models.
items.raw_summary:
In November, we outlined our approach to deprecating and preserving older Claude models.

We noted we were exploring keeping certain models available to the public post-retirement, and giving past models a way to pursue their interests.

With Claude Opus 3, we’re doing both.
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic is preserving retired Claude Opus 3 as a public model and letting past models 'pursue their interests' — a first-of-its-kind approach to model retirement and AI continuity.
items.action: Review the model preservation policy for precedent-setting implications

items.title: We’re launching Nano Banana 2, built on the latest Gemini Flash model.  🍌
items.raw_summary:
We’re launching Nano Banana 2, built on the latest Gemini Flash model.  🍌

It’s state-of-the-art for creating and editing images, combining Pro-level capabilities with lightning-fast speed. 🧵
items.why_it_matters: Google DeepMind launches Nano Banana 2, a state-of-the-art image generation model built on Gemini Flash that combines Pro-level quality with fast inference speeds.
items.action: Test Nano Banana 2 for image generation workflows

items.title: New in Cowork: scheduled tasks.
items.raw_summary:
New in Cowork: scheduled tasks.

Claude can now complete recurring tasks at specific times automatically: a morning brief, weekly spreadsheet updates, Friday team presentations. https://t.co/7ucKZbAVip

items.title: We've rolled out a new auto-memory feature.
items.raw_summary:
We've rolled out a new auto-memory feature.

Claude now remembers what it learns across sessions — your project context, debugging patterns, preferred approaches — and recalls it later without you having to write anything down. https://t.co/c7PyGaukNQ
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code's new auto-memory feature remembers project context, debugging patterns, and preferences across sessions without manual note-taking — a significant productivity multiplier.
items.action: Update Claude Code and test cross-session memory recall

items.title: Introducing Custom Agents. The AI team that never sleeps 🌙

They’re autonomous, built for teams, and easy for anyone to build. Give them a job, set a trigger or schedule, and they'll get it done 'round the clock. https://t.co/On9Jf8ISXz

items.title: Open-source game engine Godot is drowning in 'AI slop' code contributions: 'I don't know how long we
items.raw_summary:
Open-source game engine Godot is drowning in 'AI slop' code contributions: 'I don't know how long we can keep it up'

Many submissions contain nonsensical code changes, fabricated test results, and overly verbose descriptions typical of LLM output. 

Reviewing every new contributor's PR has become extremely time-consuming and demoralizing.
Rémi Verschelde stated they may not be able to sustain the current level of manual vetting much longer.
items.why_it_matters: Godot's maintainers report being overwhelmed by AI-generated slop PRs with fabricated test results, highlighting a growing crisis for open-source projects as LLM-generated contributions scale.
items.action: Review your project's PR review policies for AI-generated submissions

items.title: OpenAI, the US government and Persona built an identity surveillance machine
items.raw_summary: 53MB of source code leaked from a government endpoint. 269 verification checks. biometric face databases. SAR filings to FinCEN. and the same company that verifies your ChatGPT account. (HN: 494 pts, 159 comments)
items.why_it_matters: Leaked source code reveals a 53MB identity surveillance system built by OpenAI, the US government, and Persona with 269 verification checks and biometric databases — raising serious privacy concerns.
items.action: Read the technical analysis of the surveillance architecture

items.title: Also, one thing I'm particularly excited by: We're launching new connectors for Claude Cowork & Code
items.raw_summary:
Also, one thing I'm particularly excited by: We're launching new connectors for Claude Cowork & Code that connect Claude directly to Google Workspace (Calendar, Drive, Gmail).

Claude already runs my calendar and it's so helpful.

https://t.co/xBT1kH9Mm2
items.why_it_matters: New Google Workspace connectors for Claude Cowork and Code bring Calendar, Drive, and Gmail integration, enabling Claude to manage schedules and pull context from your work tools directly.
items.action: Connect Google Workspace to Claude for calendar and doc context

items.title: single RTX 3090. 24 GB VRAM. Qwen3.5-35B-A3B. 4-bit quant, 113 tokens per second at full 262K contex
items.raw_summary:
single RTX 3090. 24 GB VRAM. Qwen3.5-35B-A3B. 4-bit quant, 113 tokens per second at full 262K context harnessing Claude Code locally with no API, no subscription, no proxy.

told it what it is. 30 Mamba2 layers, 10 attention, 256 experts, 8 active per token. said "build something that shows off what you can do."

it visualized its own architecture. interactive. tokens flowing through layers. 256 experts lighting up on routing. served in the browser from the same GPU running inference. single pro
items.why_it_matters: Running Qwen3.5-35B-A3B at 113 tokens/sec on a single RTX 3090 at full 262K context demonstrates that local LLM inference is now viable for serious development work without API costs.
items.action: Try Qwen3.5-35B-A3B quantized on your GPU for local coding
